mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0% within a single month, tight labor market conditions trigger wage inflation expectations and consumer spending acceleration, boosting corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the monthly change in UNRATE shows no predictive power for 5-day equity returns (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a rotation from construction to manufacturing, indicating stronger corporate capacity utilization and higher profit margins, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling HOUST fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample 20-day forward return prediction (Sharpe ratio < 0.1 over 3 years), or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where the CPI index exceeds 225.0, sustained growth in real GDP compresses the output gap and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which historically suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns over 60 days are positive or neutral in >50% of high-inflation quarters despite rising GDP, or the CPIAUCSL-GDPC1 spread shows no correlation with equity valuation multiples.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales and food services directly reflects stronger consumer demand, which translates into higher near-term corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in months with rising RSAFS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 95.0, the economy operates near full capacity, leading to supply constraints that drive up input costs and compress profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of instances where INDPRO crosses above 95.0, or the monthly change in INDPRO shows no predictive power for 20-day equity returns (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in the federal funds rate signal an overheating labor market that forces monetary tightening, which historically increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling UNRATE and rising FEDFUNDS fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample 60-day forward return prediction (Sharpe ratio < 0.1 over 3 years), or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid housing market expansion where housing starts exceed 600.0, construction-related corporate earnings surge and consumer wealth effects boost discretionary spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, but only when industrial production remains stable.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns over 5 days are negative or neutral in >50% of housing boom months despite stable INDPRO, or the HOUST-INDPRO interaction shows no correlation with short-term equity momentum.
